title: Vector Length and Orthogonality Relationships
description: A visual explanation of how the inner product defines vector length (norm), distance, and the fundamental concept of orthogonality in vector spaces.
relationships: Inner product <v,v> defines the squared norm ||v||²
Norm ||v|| is the length of vector v from the origin
Distance d(u,v) = ||u - v|| is the length between vector endpoints
Vectors u and v are orthogonal if <u,v> = 0, visualized at a right angle
